name: Belief Propagation Skeleton
summary: Pass messages across a factor graph, combine them into beliefs, and retain convergence state between iterations.
dejargonized_summary: Send information between connected nodes, combine the messages into beliefs, and keep track of whether the process has stabilized.

stages.name: Variable to Factor
stages.description: Compute messages sent from variable nodes to factor nodes.
stages.dejargonized_description: Send each variable's current belief toward its neighboring factors.

stages.name: Factor to Variable
stages.description: Compute messages sent from factor nodes to variable nodes.
stages.dejargonized_description: Let each factor explain what it thinks about the neighboring variables.

stages.name: Marginal Computation
stages.dejargonized_description: Gather all of the messages and turn them into a belief for each variable.

stages.name: Memoization State
stages.description: Cache prior message state for convergence checks.
stages.dejargonized_description: Remember the prior messages so the algorithm can tell when it has stabilized.

planning_constraints.subject: belief_propagation
planning_constraints.statement: Preserve the message and memoization channels until convergence is established.
planning_constraints.rationale: Message passing relies on iterative refinement and convergence tracking.

audit.rationale: Provides a reusable message-passing scaffold for macro-level inference tasks.
audit.dejargonized_summary: Pass messages between graph neighbors, merge them into beliefs, and track whether the process has stabilized.

migration_readiness.rationale: The family scaffold is structurally stable and reusable.

checklist.description: Skeleton stages and edge semantics are explicit and loadable.
References, rationale, and dejargonized documentation are present.
The scaffold encodes a generic family pattern rather than a local shortcut.

references.title: Probabilistic Reasoning in Intelligent Systems
references.citation: Pearl, Judea. Message passing and belief propagation foundations.
